name: AI Club at CMU
description: Brings together students interested in AI and ML through workshops, speaker events, project teams, and research reading groups.

name: Roboclub
description: Builds and competes with robots. Works on everything from small autonomous vehicles to full-scale battle bots and drone systems.

name: ScottyLabs
description: Student-run org that builds tools for CMU and organizes TartanHacks, CMU's annual hackathon. Great for builders who want to ship real projects.

name: Women in Computer Science (WiCS)
description: Supports women and gender minorities in CS at CMU through mentorship programs, industry events, workshops, and community building.

name: Design for America
description: Uses human-centered design to address local and social challenges. Works with community partners on real-world design projects.

name: HackCMU
description: Organizes CMU's student-run hackathon. Open to all skill levels — a great way to build something in 24 hours and meet other builders.

name: CMU Entrepreneurship Club
description: Connects aspiring entrepreneurs with mentors, investors, and resources. Hosts pitch competitions, speaker panels, and startup workshops.

name: CMU ACM Chapter
description: CMU's chapter of the Association for Computing Machinery. Hosts competitive programming contests, technical workshops, and career prep events.

name: Data Science Club
description: Explores data science through projects, workshops, and Kaggle competitions. Open to all levels from beginners to experienced practitioners.

name: Society of Women Engineers (SWE)
description: Supports women in engineering through professional development, mentorship, outreach, and building a supportive community at CMU.

name: CMU Environmental Coalition
description: Advocates for environmental sustainability on campus and beyond. Organizes policy discussions, sustainability projects, and climate action events.

name: PRISM (CS + Social Good)
description: Explores the intersection of computing and social impact. Works on projects and discussions around tech policy, AI ethics, and using CS for good.

name: Tartan Racing
description: CMU's autonomous vehicle team. Works on self-driving car technology and competes in national autonomous vehicle challenges.

name: CMU HCI Student Group
description: Community for students interested in HCI, UX research, and design. Shares resources, hosts design critiques, and connects students with HCI faculty.

name: CMU Finance Club
description: Covers investment banking, quantitative finance, and financial markets through workshops, case studies, and industry speaker events.
